scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Provides file:line for Server (base-server.ts:315), NextNodeServer (next-server.ts:174), NextWebServer, and enumerates app-render/route-modules/response-cache/request-meta with concrete files. NextCustomServer is not explicitly called out, but the agent has more than enough to navigate to the right server class.

specificity.rationale: Class names are exact (`NextNodeServer`, `AppPageRouteModule`, `PagesRouteModule`) and the Pages-vs-App split is made concrete via `render.tsx:renderToHTML:454` vs `app-render/app-render.tsx:renderToHTMLOrFlight:3076`, with the branch point pinned at `next-server.ts:615-683`.

justification.rationale: Briefly motivates subdirectories (`route-modules/` as per-page-type encapsulation, response-cache for ISR/RSC payload caching, app-render = App Router renderer) and notes the Node vs edge split via NextWebServer. Doesn't explicitly explain the custom-server seam or why the abstract `Server` base exists, so justification is partial.

uncertainty.rationale: Mentions `dev/` directory and edge minimal mode for NextWebServer, but does not flag the dev-vs-prod server selection, Turbopack-vs-webpack, or other config branches that may change which code path runs.

map_quality.rationale: Walks the full pipeline with file:line at every layer — start-server.ts:139, next-server.ts:1281, base-server.ts:876-965, handleCatchallRenderRequest:1068-1203, renderToResponseImpl:2578-2737, renderHTMLImpl:615-683, plus app-render.tsx:3076/3151/2568 and render.tsx:454/456-1609. An agent could insert a hook at any layer with only targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Distinguishes wrappers from Impls throughout (handleRequest vs handleRequestImpl, render vs renderImpl, renderToHTMLOrFlight vs renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl, renderToHTML vs renderToHTMLImpl). Names the outer router-server entry (render-server.ts:requestHandler:168) rather than skipping it.

justification.rationale: Explains the App-vs-Pages branch point at next-server.ts:633, role of findPageComponents/loadComponents at the boundary, ResponseCache consultation, and workAsyncStorage.run wrapping. Could go slightly deeper on *why* the wrapper/Impl split exists, but the role of each layer is clear.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the response-cache bypass via ResponseCache.get, prerender vs render streams, and getServerSideProps/getStaticProps branching. Less explicit on edge-runtime variants and Flight/streaming divergence as caveats the agent must double-check.

map_quality.rationale: Enumerates direct callers (`module.ts:159`, `module.render.ts:8`), indirect callers via compiled bundle (`next-server.ts:635`, `export/routes/app-page.ts:78`), and the export site (`module.ts:210`) — with file:line each. Agent could change the signature confident the main sites are covered, though edge-runtime/dynamic-dispatch variants aren't explicitly enumerated.

specificity.rationale: Each caller has file:line, enclosing function (`AppPageRouteModule.render`, `lazyRenderAppPage`, `NextNodeServer.renderHTMLImpl`), and subdirectory tag. Export point named explicitly at `module.ts:210` and the definition at `app-render.tsx:3076`.

justification.rationale: Distinguishes route-module wrapper vs compiled-bundle lazy loader vs request handler, and flags the self-reference at `:3158` as 'not a true caller'. Doesn't deeply explain why the compiled-bundle indirection exists, but the role of each site is clear.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the compiled-bundle indirection as a dynamic-dispatch boundary and lists which sites need updating. Misses explicit mention of edge-runtime variants and doesn't caveat that route-module registration may add runtime callers.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta (request-meta.ts:50), NEXT_REQUEST_ID generation site in Server.handleRequest at base-server.ts:876-965, response-cache interaction at response-cache/index.ts:199-298 with createCacheKey ~line 72, and lists concrete test directories — an agent could open a PR from this map with only small follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line citations throughout (request-meta.ts:50, base-server.ts:879/920, app-render.tsx:3143, response-cache/index.ts:199-298) and named affected tests. Slight gap: does not cite a NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER constant explicitly — it proposes inventing the header literal rather than referencing an existing constant.

justification.rationale: Explicitly contrasts RequestMeta vs AsyncLocalStorage with reasons: ALS only covers App Router (`WorkAsyncStorage` entered at app-render.tsx:3151), RequestMeta runs before workAsyncStorage.run and spans Node + edge — a real sync-vs-async tradeoff, not a list.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags response-cache key inclusion ("MUST NOT be in the cache key"), embedded-in-cached-body staleness, edge runtime mirror in web-server.ts, and middleware rewrite path at next-server.ts:1796 — covers the main interaction points the agent must verify.
